title: PE character varieties and orderability for Dehn fillings of knot complements
speaker: Marc Culler

proof_stages.label: Orderability, taut foliations, and L-spaces
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins by defining left orderability and placing it in the three-manifold framework relating orderable fundamental groups, coorientable taut foliations, and non-L-space rational homology spheres via known theorems and the Boyer-Gordon-Watson conjecture.

proof_stages.label: Orderable Dehn fillings near the longitude
proof_stages.summary: The central theorem is stated for a homology-circle knot exterior with incompressibility and Alexander-polynomial hypotheses, asserting an interval of orderable Dehn fillings around longitude filling, and it is compared with Roberts’s taut-foliation interval and with the L-space filling cone predicted by Floer theory.

proof_stages.label: PE character varieties and the pillowcase
proof_stages.summary: The speaker sets up the representation and character varieties, the real locus, the boundary torus pillowcase, and the peripherally elliptic character variety, using the hypotheses to force one-dimensional components, exclude ideal-point problems for longitude, and separate SU(2), SL(2,R), and peripheral-real X-characters.

proof_stages.label: Alexander-root transition to real representations
proof_stages.summary: A simple unit-circle root of the Alexander polynomial gives a reducible nonabelian representation which deforms into adjacent SU(2) and SL(2,R) arcs, with the local dimension control coming from cohomology and the Alexander matrix.

proof_stages.label: Translation numbers and the Dehn-filling criterion
proof_stages.summary: For PE SL(2,R) representations the peripheral action lifts to the universal cover of SL(2,R) and hence to the line, so normalized meridian and longitude translation numbers form arcs whose intersections with Dehn-filling lines yield faithful line actions and therefore orderable filled groups.

proof_stages.label: Real places of trace fields as another source of arcs
proof_stages.summary: A second mechanism is described: a real place of the trace field of a hyperbolic knot complement Galois-conjugates the holonomy to a real representation near a parabolic point, producing SL(2,R) arcs and orderability for one tail of sufficiently large positive or negative slopes.

proof_stages.label: Computational limitations and exceptional components
proof_stages.summary: Computed translation diagrams show both the reach and the limitations of the SL(2,R)-representation method, including gaps relative to the expected L-space boundary, parabolic-to-parabolic arcs, X-representation phenomena, and constant-trace components arising from bending along essential tori.
